data.content_ref: inline:The claim attributes the collapse to new infantry tactics overwhelming chariot aristocracies, but the scope includes 'Sea Peoples' as a covered entity while excluding 'Egypt's core Nile valley.' This creates a logical inconsistency: historical records (e.g., Merneptah and Ramesses III inscriptions) explicitly detail Egypt defeating these very Sea Peoples using similar infantry/chariot combinations. If the 'new tactics' were sufficient to overwhelm the Hittite and Mycenaean elites, the mechanism fails to explain why these same tactics failed against the Egyptian elite, unless the argument relies on Egyptian exceptionalism not accounted for in the mechanism's description of tactical superiority.
